title: Golden Brown Canadian Chicken Toast
description: A malty, bouncy-qq, crowd-pleaser Canadian dish featuring chicken bouillon powder, served in a Korean brass bowl, this paleo-friendly recipe is perfect for a quick lunch or snack. It combines the richness of chicken with the crunch of toasted bread.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a small bowl, mix together chicken bouillon powder, sea salt, black pepper, garlic powder, and onion powder.
Brush the paleo-friendly bread slices with coconut oil on both sides.
Sprinkle the chicken bouillon powder mixture evenly over the bread slices.
Place the bread slices on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the bread in the preheated oven for 10 minutes or until golden brown.
While the bread is baking, slice the chicken breast into thin strips.
Remove the bread from the oven and top each slice with a chicken strip.
Return the bread to the oven and bake for an additional 5 minutes or until the chicken is cooked through.
